author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Added complex matrix support via a dedicated ferray-linalg/src/complex.rs module. Took the pragmatic path rather than extending LinalgFloat — making LinalgFloat generic over both real and complex would require touching every generic function in the crate (matmul_2d, solve, inv, decomp/*, norms::*, products::*, etc. — 20+ sites all threading the scalar/complex split through the bounds). Adding a small set of complex-specific entry points keeps the real-number fast path clean and delivers the headline feature without the blast radius.

faer 0.24 does the heavy lifting: its ComplexField impl for Complex<T> (via impl<T: RealField<Unit: ComplexField>> ComplexField for Complex<T>) means faer::Mat<Complex<f64>> and faer::Mat<Complex<f32>> both work unchanged — the ferray layer is just the element-type bridge.

New functions, all in the complex namespace and re-exported at the crate root:
- matmul_complex(a, b) — complex matrix multiply
- inv_complex(a) — complex matrix inverse via partial-pivot LU
- solve_complex(a, b) — A x = b for a 2-D complex RHS
- solve_complex_vec(a, b) — same but with a 1-D RHS vector
- det_complex(a) — complex matrix determinant

All generic over Complex<T> where T is Copy + 1-static and Complex<T>: Element + ComplexField, which resolves to both Complex<f32> and Complex<f64> for free.

11 new unit tests cover: 2x2 matmul with identity, 2x2 matmul with non-trivial values (hand-verified), shape mismatch rejection, inverse of identity and diag(i, 2i), solve with identity RHS (2-D and vector forms), complex determinant of diag(2+i, 3-i), the roundtrip A @ inv(A) = I, and non-square rejection for det/inv. 120 lib tests (up from 109) + 21 oracle + 12 property pass. Clippy clean.

Eigendecomposition, SVD, and batched complex ops are deferred — callers that need those can cast to real via the existing eig which already returns Complex outputs. This pass closes the biggest gap (basic complex linear algebra).
